summary: The diff implements a chunked image storage approach, but rather than using the explicit uploadImageChunk/finalizeImage Motoko endpoints described in REQ-19, it introduces an 'ExternalBlob' abstraction (blob-storage component) that handles chunking internally. The backend main.mo no longer stores raw Base64 strings (image fields are Storage.ExternalBlob types), and the frontend uses ExternalBlob.fromBytes() with upload progress. However, several specific requirements from REQ-19 and REQ-20 are not clearly met or are missing.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend exposes an explicit uploadImageChunk(inviteId, fieldKey, chunkIndex, totalChunks, chunkData) endpoint in the Motoko actor
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows backend/main.mo includes a MixinStorage mixin but the truncated content does not show any explicit uploadImageChunk public function with the specified signature (inviteId, fieldKey, chunkIndex, totalChunks, chunkData).

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend exposes an explicit finalizeImage(inviteId, fieldKey) endpoint that reassembles chunks and stores the result in the invite record
missing_requirements.reason: No explicit finalizeImage function is visible in the backend/main.mo diff. The blob-storage mixin may handle this internally, but the requirement specifies a named finalizeImage endpoint tied to inviteId + fieldKey that updates the invite record's image fields.

missing_requirements.requirement: Chunk reassembly handles out-of-order delivery by using chunkIndex ordering
missing_requirements.reason: The blob-storage mixin internals are not shown in the diff. There is no visible evidence of chunkIndex-based ordering logic in the shown code.

missing_requirements.requirement: Selecting and uploading a bride, groom, or cover photo triggers chunked upload calls to the backend and finalizes the image before the user proceeds
missing_requirements.reason: StepPhotoUpload.tsx calls ExternalBlob.fromBytes().withUploadProgress() but there is no evidence of actual backend canister calls being made during photo selection. The blob is stored in form state but it is unclear when or if it actually uploads to the canister before the user proceeds.

missing_requirements.requirement: The publish payload sent via createInvite/updateInvite no longer contains raw Base64 image strings
missing_requirements.reason: The frontend passes ExternalBlob instances in the payload, but how ExternalBlob serializes when passed to the Candid interface is not shown. If it embeds Base64 data inline, the 413 problem would persist.

hallucinated_features.description: A migration.mo module that converts old InviteRecords with string image fields to new InviteRecords with Storage.ExternalBlob image fields, dropping image data
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention any migration module or data migration strategy. This is an extra file added beyond the stated requirements.

hallucinated_features.description: ExternalBlob abstraction with fromBytes(), fromURL(), getDirectURL(), and withUploadProgress() methods as a shared frontend/backend type
hallucinated_features.reason: The requirements specify uploadImageChunk and finalizeImage as explicit named endpoints. Instead, an ExternalBlob class abstraction was introduced that encapsulates this behavior differently from what was specified.

hallucinated_features.description: MixinStorage pattern (blob-storage/Mixin and blob-storage/Storage modules) included in the Motoko actor
hallucinated_features.reason: The requirements specify adding uploadImageChunk and finalizeImage as direct endpoints in the single main actor. The mixin pattern is an architectural choice not mentioned in the build request.
